programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: Mark treats a beautiful amnesiac, unaware she is actually a hired assassin who has infiltrated the hospital to kill influential senator Robert Bassett, who has been admitted for surgery. Medical detective drama, starring Dick Van Dyke

programme.title: Diagnosis Murder
programme.desc: The abrasive, unpopular host of a money-raising telethon is murdered live on air, and it is not long before suspicion falls on his former writers. Medical detective drama, guest starring Dom DeLuise, with Dick Van Dyke and Victoria Rowell

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: CD is haunted by an unsolved 11-year-old murder case just before he is about to be inducted into the Texas Ranger Hall of Fame

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Drug manufacturing equipment is found in the apartment of a murdered woman - and the discovery that she was wearing a concealed microphone leads Horatio and the team to an unscrupulous FBI agent with a secret life. Drama, starring David Caruso, Khandi Alexander and Jonathan Togo

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the murder of a Navy commander's wife, determining whether it is linked to her role as a therapist, or an act of terrorism since her husband's name has appeared on a Jihadist's target list. Elsewhere, the group discusses Halloween costumes, as well as Tony's recent dating habits. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part one of two. The agents investigate when two men are killed at a gun shop, but soon discover one of their colleagues could be at risk. programme.title: The Fugitive

programme.title: Perry Mason
programme.desc: An attempt to commit a wealthy man to a mental institution brings a new kind of challenge to Perry, especially when the case is shattered by murder.

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: An internal security breach forces NCIS to go into lockdown when a covert listening device is found in a pen belonging to Secretary of the Navy Sarah Porter. DiNozzo discovers that National Security Agency analyst Ellie Bishop wrote a report two years previously predicting the possibility of such an occurrence and she is brought in to assist with the investigation and track down the culprit. Guest starring Leslie Hope and Emily Wickersham

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: A formidable foe means Walker's karate skills are put into practice

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A former serviceman who had been having an affair is beaten to death in his own back garden, and the agents are forced to consider the possibility that his wife - a US Marine and martial arts expert - is the killer. Meanwhile, McGee becomes the subject of an embarrassing mix-up when an inflatable doll apparently purchased on his credit card is delivered. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Part two of two. The Chameleon's planted clues lead the team on a wild goose chase as it searches for NSA agent Mike Atley, who the criminal claims to have in his custody. Callen is faced with a difficult decision when he is told to release the Chameleon in order to save Atley's life, and contemplates subduing his nemesis once and for all

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when marine corporal Flegman is found shot to death, and a phone leads them to a hotel room where Gibbs awkwardly stumbles upon his ex-wife Diane Sterling and her FBI agent ex-husband TC Fornell. She had been attempting to call her boyfriend Eddie to break up with him, but somehow Flegman had taken possession of his phone. The team tracks down Eddie's car and discovers a clown nose along with traces of blood and he quickly becomes the prime suspect

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a billionaire philanthropist, who has been found dead in his study. However, what should be a relatively simple case is complicated by the discovery that the victim's widow is Horatio's old flame Julia - the mother of his convict son Kyle. Guest starring Elizabeth Berkley, with David Caruso and Emily Procter

programme.title: The Fugitive
programme.desc: Kimble witnesses an accidental killing and tries to save an innocent man from being charged with murder.

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Sylvester seriously injures himself after triggering an explosive device during an investigation into a gang of thieves. The team must work with Interpol and remain focused on finding the culprits while their friend is in a critical condition. Meanwhile, Drew decides he wants to get close to Paige again. Drama, inspired by a true story, starring Ari Stidham and Brendan Hines

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: The team races against the clock to save a boy who is trapped inside a sinkhole. Walter manages to get Megan into a study researching a new MS drug, but she is not keen on being part of a trial. Meanwhile, Paige tries to bring the festive spirit to the team when Drew is forced to miss Christmas with Ralph for a baseball try-out in Portland. Drama, starring Elyes Gabel, Eddie Kaye Thomas and Camille Guaty

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Navy lieutenant Paul Booth is shot dead while sitting on a park bench. It turns out that the last people he called were his boss, his wife and Penelope Langston - a rebellious academic who also happens to be McGee's grandmother. She says that the victim was an old student of hers who kept in touch, but later reveals she was recruited to work on a special project during the Vietnam War by the head of a top-secret think tank Booth was a member of. Guest starring Lily Tomlin, with Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A man who died after being knocked down by a van is revealed to be a professional hitman responsible for at least six contract killings - and his next target was to be a navy lieutenant commander. Abby is delighted at being a match for a teenager who needs a kidney transplant, but she is informed that someone else with very similar DNA has also been identified as suitable. Has she found a relative she never knew about?

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The murder of a coastguard officer on board an abandoned cargo ship draws the team's attention to a family of Lebanese refugees hoping to find a new home in America. Meanwhile, Tony and McGee try to find the perfect woman for Gibbs. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon and Sean Murray

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The body of a Navy lieutenant is found on the side of a remote road and toxicology tests reveal he died of overhydration. When another man is found dead nearby, the NCIS team begins to suspect a serial killer is on the loose. Meanwhile, Ducky introduces Gibbs to his new girlfriend. Crime drama, guest starring Cheryl Ladd

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Homeland Security employee Victor Sterling mysteriously disappears and his car is found at a drive-through restaurant that becomes the scene of a double murder. To complicate matters further, the missing man's outspoken spouse happens to be the ex-wife of both Gibbs and Fornell. American naval crime drama, starring Mark Harmon, Joe Spano, Tom Gallop and Melinda McGraw

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Part two of two. The safe house is attacked, leaving Vance in hospital and Eli missing, with his bodyguard Amit Hadar dead. The movements of all the Palestinians are accounted for at the time of the attack, making it unclear who was responsible, and Ziva suspects her Mossad replacement Liat is hiding something. Drama, starring Michael Nouri, Cote de Pablo, Rocky Carroll and Mark Harmon

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A Navy officer scheduled to testify in a forthcoming murder trial goes missing after receiving a threat at the safe house where he was staying, and the deputy district attorney asks the NCIS agents to locate him. One of their few leads is a dead turkey Tony and McGee find in the man's fridge, so they take it to Ducky for an autopsy. Crime drama, starring Sean Murray and Michael Weatherly

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The agents investigate the murder of a marine during a cruise. They discover he came from a famous and wealthy family but had turned his back on a life of privilege to join the military, and after the death of his father had inherited a large part of his business. His decision to change his will may have pushed one of his relatives to kill him to keep the firm in their control. Crime drama, guest-starring Diane Neal (Law & Order: Special Victims Unit)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US Navy recruitment officer is murdered at a high school and the agents discover he had recently begun a relationship with a married teacher whose wife held a grudge against him. They also find out a student's father was vehemently opposed to his joining the military, and receive help from an unexpected quarter - Ducky's esteemed predecessor, Dr Magnus. Crime drama, guest starring comedian Bob Newhart

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The NCIS team is assigned to a joint terrorism task force by the Secretary when a Moscow-born scientist who it is believed may have been targeted by the Russians for selling state secrets, is found dead. However, when she too is found dead, the team discovers the hallmarks of Sergei Mishnev, the terrorist who nearly killed Gibbs. Starring Mark Harmon, Michael Weatherly and Pauley Perrette

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The Saint Marie Literary Festival is rocked by the death of a young literature student - and Florence's childhood friend - whose body is discovered at the bottom of a cliff. Suicide seems the likely cause, but as ever Humphrey isn't so keen to jump to conclusions - even with the presence of a note in the victim's bag. But with everyone the dead girl knew at the festival, who could have pushed her over the edge? Away from the investigation, the inspector is busy preparing the shack for Martha's stay. Sun-soaked whodunit, guest starring Fiona Allen, Ramon Tikaram and Monica Dolan

programme.desc: An armoured security van is discovered at the bottom of a lake, and the team makes a link to a disappearance 17 years previously and an unsolved killing, prompting them to reopen the case. Meanwhile, Harry meets Wade's sister Miriam and agrees to help find her brother's killer. programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Smithy upsets the Longmarsh heavies by spurning their offer of protection and is beaten up for his pains, but it is the approach of Louise's funeral that drives him to despair. Dan Casper enters a supposedly empty flat where he is surprised to find a naked woman, and her confused explanation of how she came to be there finally gives the Sun Hill detectives a lead in the Pete Larson case. Bill Treacher guest stars

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. Smithy is distraught when he's refused permission to attend Louise's funeral and loses control when he finds fellow prisoner Marty Johnson setting fire to a photograph of her. Jack approaches the CPS with evidence of the link between Pete Larson and Carl Caplan, but Keane will only grant CID 24 hours to put the suspect under surveillance and come up with concrete proof that the sergeant was framed

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part one of two. Feelings are running high following the death of Jordan Tomlin and Okaro drafts in a full team to police a sell-out gig at the Bombastic nightclub. Forged tickets swell the crowd to dangerous levels and the punters turn nasty when Emma Keane is forced to arrest a man for possession of drugs. Faced with a potential riot outside, Yvonne Hemmingway makes a bad call which leads to disaster

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Part two of two. The aftermath of the nightclub disaster leaves the Sun Hill relief in shock and Adam Okaro summons all the self-control he can muster to set aside personal tragedy before meeting Met Press Officer Mia Perry. Will Fletcher and Tony Stamp face a traumatic morning trying to identify the bodies in a temporary morgue, and Emma Keane reunites a mother with her dying son

programme.title: Peak Practice
programme.desc: Andrew returns to Cardale after six months away and asks for his old job back. The others are furious at being given the runaround, having endured months of working with below-par locums, and consider appointing a rival candidate. On the personal front, Will is over the moon at becoming a father _ but there's a dark cloud on the horizon. Rural medical drama, starring Simon Shepherd, Saskia Wickham, Gary Mavers and Adrian Lukis

programme.title: Catherine Cookson's The Secret
programme.desc: Feature-length dramatisation of the writer's only thriller, set in 19th-century Tyneside. Colin Buchanan stars as successful businessman Freddie Musgrave, who has worked hard for 20 years to live down his misspent youth as runner to a gang of smugglers and is shocked when a face appears from long ago, threatening to reveal the details of his sordid past. With Clare Higgins and June Whitfield

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: A rare disease spreads around Poplar Street, and Patsy and Dr Turner try to trace the outbreak to its source. Suspicion over the identity of the first carrier threatens to divide a previously close-knit family. Sister Winifred is forced to confront her prejudices while treating a patient, and Barbara worries about how Trixie will react when she agrees to go to dinner with Tom

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne is seconded to a maternity ward, and is impressed by how modern technology is being used to help the mothers - but is left with a moral dilemma after assisting on a traumatic birth. A young man from Poplar is delighted to be accepted into university, but his mother is less than happy. His dreams are jeopardised when his girlfriend gets pregnant, a development that puts pressure on the whole family

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Richard is forced to think on his feet when he forgets his wedding anniversary and tells Hyacinth he has arranged to have a security alarm fitted as a present. Social-climbing comedy, starring Patricia Routledge and Clive Swift

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth plans an extravagant riverside picnic and invites all and sundry from the higher echelons of society, including the vicar and his wife. Alas for her dreams of social success, the event proves a wash-out. Patricia Routledge stars

programme.title: Keeping Up Appearances
programme.desc: Hyacinth disappoints Richard by buying him a pair of skis for his birthday, intending to strap them to the car in yet another attempt to impress the neighbours. However, the scheme backfires when her rich sister Violet arrives and cuts her down to size

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Michelle Collins guest stars as a company boss who brings her staff to Saint Marie on an outdoor team-building exercise - although she isn't around for long as she gets bumped off in her shack. Humphrey tries to work out who is responsible - the aggrieved assistant, the long-lost son, the sister whose husband the victim slept with - or someone else. All is going well, until a shock confession brings the investigation to an abrupt halt - and an unexpected phone call from home only adds to the inspector's problems. With Kaye Wragg, Jason Merrells, Sylvestra Le Touzel, Matthew Lewis and Gary Lewis

Crime thriller, starring John Hannah and Eilidh MacDonald

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The freak wave that capsized the boat of two young men attempting to set a world record rowing the Atlantic. Plus, the family day out in the snow that almost ended in tragedy when a man suffered an accident while sledging

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on meat, cooking a rack of Scotch lamb with roast aubergine, with guests sheep farmer Michelle Stephen and comedian Jim Smith

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the FA Cup final between Arsenal and Chelsea at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session on the second day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Mark King and John Higgins v Matthew Stevens. Ding plays his match to a finish, while four-time champion Higgins, runner-up for the last three years and always one of the men to beat, gets his campaign under way

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Chelsea (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the showpiece match from Wembley Stadium. The Gunners recorded a 2-0 victory over holders Manchester City in the semi-finals thanks to a brace by Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, and Chelsea produced an impressive performance in a 3-1 victory over Manchester United the following day. The sides also met in the 2017 final, when Arsenal prevailed 2-1, though more recently, Chelsea won 4-1 against their London rivals in the 2019 Europa League final. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces from the world of food and drink to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are Ken Hom, Ching-He Huang, Candice Brown, Atul Kochhar, Andi Oliver, Michael O'Hare, Matt Tebbutt and Olly Smith

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: David is excited for Rosa to return home after visiting Xiomara, but he starts to worry that his strict routine may be boring for her. Jacob is concerned when Nate is brought into the ED struggling to breathe. Masters guiltily remembers that he should have picked up Nate's medication as soon as the interview was over. And when Lev's family life takes a turn for the worst, he battles temptation and wanders into the arms of a man he's been messaging. Starring Jason Durr, Uriel Emil and Charles Venn

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the second match in a three-game ODI series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Prior to the opening contest in the series, England had won their last five one-dayers at this venue, twice defeating Pakistan and the West Indies and also recording a victory over South Africa

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Ellie is a fashion fanatic looking for a guy who shares her passion for clothes and shoes - but will she see past the clothes with Mr Grim, Mr Snaps or Mr Waves

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Katie likes an older man who can keep her on her toes - and magicians, motorbikes and a didgeridoo are just some of the things that she has to look forward to

programme.title: Spike Island (2012)
programme.desc: Five teenage boys from Manchester are desperate to see the Stone Roses in concert during the summer of 1990. Making it to the gig at Cheshire's Spike Island means spending three days on the road, an experience that tests their friendships and forces them to consider what their future holds. Drama, starring Elliott Tittensor and Nico Mirallegro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A meadow in Woodville in Derbyshire, an old mill in Stoke in Staffordshire and a terraced house in Mountain Ash in Wales are all sold under the hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help teams look for items at the Mona Showground in Anglesey, while presenter Anita Manning finds out about Portmeirion pottery

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore visits Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland. Rupert Blanchard works on wooden garden chairs, while Bruce Faulseit turns a sewing machine stand into something special

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a Scouts-inspired garden, complete with campfire and winding path, for Thomas and Kate in Harborne, Birmingham

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein reinvestigate the 1884 murder of a policeman in Co Durham, a crime for which three men were tried but only one was hanged

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The pups embark on a foraging mission, but find themselves in trouble when a giant eagle owl appears in search of a meal, and a thunderstorm threatens the family

programme.title: A Wild Year
programme.desc: The seasons are marked on Wales' Pembrokeshire's coast by the arrival of the puffins, which court by tapping their multi-coloured bills. Farmers grow the earliest potatoes, while sheep are employed to maintain the clifftop pastures for the rare choughs that use their long red beaks to probe for grubs. As autumn arrives, the grey seals produce their white-coated pups just before the gales mark the might of winter. Using time-lapse techniques, this documentary brings fresh insight into the restless rhythms of the countryside in an exploration of how and why this region is the way it is. Narrated by Toby Jones

programme.title: Lost Land of the Jaguar
programme.desc: The team travels deeper into the Guyanan jungle and scorpions and poisonous centipedes enter the base camp. Gordon Buchanan discovers an animal is stealing supplies and Steve Backshall finds giant piranha and long-fanged sabretooth fish in the river. Meanwhile, George McGavin catches an enormous tarantula and the climbing team tries to scale a huge unclimbed mountain at the forest's edge

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: First-round coverage at the Crucible Theatre, with Judd Trump beginning the defence of his title against Tom Ford, and Stuart Bingham also in action against Ashley Carty

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: First-round coverage continues at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where two more matches get under way in the showpiece tournament on the snooker calendar. This is also one of the highlights of the sporting year, which, like so many events in 2020, is taking place in vastly different circumstances to the norm. Several promising overseas players have been unable to enter the tournament, but former runner-up Ding Junhui is among those taking part, and he begins his campaign this afternoon against Mark King

programme.title: Eggheads
programme.desc: Jeremy Vine hosts the quiz in which the winners of famous game shows work as a team to tackle a new set of challengers hoping to win a cash prize

programme.title: Meerkat Manor
programme.desc: The family is caught in a storm and forced to take shelter in a rival group's den - which causes a ruckus when the inhabitants return. Meanwhile, group leader Flower evicts one of the young females in an aggressive fashion

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session gets under way at the Crucible Theatre, where world number one and defending champion Judd Trump takes on Tom Ford, and Mark Williams begins his match against Alan McManus. Trump has enjoyed an outstanding season and is looking to break the 'Crucible Curse', and become the only first-time world champion to successfully defend his title at the first time of asking

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don refreshes a container of overgrown herbs, plants alpines in a pot and revisits the shrub and perennial cuttings he took a few weeks ago. Arit Anderson travels to Kent to meet a designer who is placing sustainability and the environment at the heart of the gardens she designs. Plus, a garden in Dorset that has been laid out specifically with scent in mind and an archive report by Frances Tophill who last year visited an allotment on the Isle of Man to meet a grower who uses plants to make cosmetics and there's a second chance to meet an enthusiast on the Isle of Wight who has filled his garden with daylilies

programme.title: Miriam Margolyes: Almost Australian
programme.desc: On the second leg of her journey, Miriam is intrigued by the Australian concept of 'mateship' and how Australia is the only country in the world to enshrine this in its culture. Her first stop is a roadhouse south of Alice Springs, where she meets one of the few women truckers in Australia. She also encounters Aboriginal elders, who set up the Children's Ground to pass Arrernte traditions, songs, dances and stories on to the younger generation. Arriving in Darwin, Miriam encounters a group of transgender Tiwi islanders, who invite her to a drag night

programme.title: I Love
programme.desc: Holly Johnson looks back at the year when the charts were dominated by Nik Kershaw, Madonna, Sade and his own band Frankie Goes to Hollywood, whose song Relax caused a storm of controversy. He reveals how Brat Pack stars Demi Moore, Rob Lowe and Emilio Estevez shot to fame, while Robin of Sherwood and The A-Team hit TV screens, the Care Bears melted young hearts and Trivial Pursuit sparked a new era in board games

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Tom tries talking Louise into helping him with a silly lie, but presenting a united front proves tricky. Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Mark Urban present analysis of the day's national and international events

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on the opening day, where Judd Trump v Tom Ford reached a conclusion, and Mark Williams v Alan McManus got under way

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: An in-depth focus on the opening day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, as the season-ending championship got under way with the best-of-19-frames first-round matches

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: What began with a handful of mystery pneumonia cases in Wuhan late last year has now left more than half a million dead worldwide. Beijing says it's been open and transparent throughout, but former BBC China editor Carrie Gracie examines how it delayed reporting the initial outbreak and evidence that Coronavirus could be spread by people, andalso silenced doctors who tried to speak out. She hears from one high-level insider who believes the animal market at the centre of the Wuhan outbreak should have been treated as a crime scene, and from experts who warn that this crisis may be a dress rehearsal for an even more deadly pandemic in the future

programme.title: The Rise of the Murdoch Dynasty
programme.desc: Three-part documentary about the media mogul's influence on international politics and the power struggles within his own family. The first episode focuses on the build-up to the 1997 general election, where Rupert Murdoch had to decide if his business interests were best served by the continuation of John Major's struggling government, or a new Labour leadership under Tony Blair. Eventually, the full force of his media empire is placed behind the rising Labour leader, but some question how high a price was paid. Elsewhere, Murdoch marries a woman 37 years younger than he is, dividing his children and jeopardising his plans to choose a successor

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Marrying Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Sisters Rosie and Bea plan revenge on their vegetable-loving parents

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Strange places where animals can set up home, including penguins nesting in a garden, pigs swimming in the sea, and a fish living inside the bottom of a sea cucumber

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Ed Petrie and friends take part in a singing contest at Porthcawl's Elvis Festival

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: A young boy called Aron loves all things Bollywood, especially dancing, so the team builds him his very own Bollywood-style den

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve meets baby orangutans and is strangled by a python on a night-time river expedition

programme.title: Mountain Gorilla
programme.desc: Patrick Stewart narrates this documentary, following the last remaining members of the species as they fight for survival on the slopes of central Africa. Dominant silverback Cantsbee protects those close to him as they make a perilous descent to feed on bamboo. On the other side of the Rwandan volcanos, a young female gorilla looks to her father for guidance after being deserted by her mother, and a teenage silverback tries to strike out on his own in Uganda

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The second day's play in Sheffield gets under way, where former champion Stuart Bingham plays his match at the Crucible Theatre to a finish against Ashley Carty, and last year's semi-finalist David Gilbert begins his campaign against Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Nigellissima
programme.desc: Nigella Lawson demonstrates how to prepare a range of straightforward yet sumptuous Italian dishes, including her recipe for roast chicken with saffron orzotto. She also creates two desserts - coffee ice-cream and a chocolate olive-oil cake - and a pasta dish with courgettes that is ideal for late-night snacking

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk samples Punjabi food at a restaurant that has hosted the royals on more than one occasion. He tries a traditional tandoori salmon, which Prince Charles was wowed by

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: Len takes actress Lisa Riley - best known to Emmerdale fans as Mandy Dingle on a journey to Bronte Country - Haworth in West Yorkshire - to re-create a family trip there in 1992. The pair travel in a Citroen CX, just like the one Lisa's mum drove all those years ago, and after the obligatory Barbra Streisand singalong, Lisa explains the importance of the Bronte sisters on her childhood. Along the way, Len and Lisa take a trip on a steam train, visit the Bronte memorial chapel and chat about the actress's career

programme.title: The Railway Children (1970)
programme.desc: Three Edwardian children are forced to leave their comfortable middle-class London home and move to the wilds of Yorkshire after their father is wrongfully imprisoned for treason. Family drama adapted from E Nesbit's story, starring Jenny Agutter, Sally Thomsett, Gary Warren, Dinah Sheridan, Bernard Cribbins, William Mervyn and Iain Cuthbertson

programme.title: The Great British Countryside
programme.desc: Hugh Dennis and Julia Bradbury explore Yorkshire, from the Moors and Dales to the coast. The comedian takes the controls of a glider and gets winched down into one of Britain's largest potholes. Meanwhile, Julia discovers how the county's rocks have turned Agatha Christie's handbag to stone, and reveals how they produce mineral water

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Further coverage of the first round from Sheffield, where Ding Junhui and Mark King are looking to book a place in the last 16, and John Higgins takes on Matthew Stevens

programme.title: David Attenborough's Natural Curiosities
programme.desc: The evolutionary documentary concludes, as David Attenborough profiles the Siamese fighting fish, so aggressive it will fight its own reflection until it is exhausted. He also looks at the kangaroo - once pitted against humans in the boxing ring - which has a kick so strong it can kill

programme.title: Amazing Hotels: Life Beyond the Lobby
programme.desc: Monica Galetti and Giles Coren visit Schloss Elmau, a hotel located deep in the snowy Bavarian Alps that is promoted as providing a cultural hideaway that allows guests the chance to restore their mind, body and soul. Monica joins in one of the meditation sessions held every day for the restaurant staff, and Giles recites poetry and travels to a mountain hut to serve dinner, while dressed in lederhosen. They join the hotel guests for an evening performance featuring world-renowned musicians playing for free

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with a 1936 edition of Bradshaw's Continental Railway Guide, Michael Portillo ventures onto the European rail network, beginning an emotional rail journey which takes him deep into his family's past. In the golden city of Salamanca in northwestern Spain, Michael visits the University to hear of opposition to the fascist takeover of Spain by General Francisco Franco, and gains access to the General's archive of enemies of the state. Travelling onto the capital city of Madrid, Michael hears how the bombing of a small town in the Basque region in 1937 inspired one of the 20th century's most shocking works of art, before making his way to the town of Huesca. Michael's final stop is on the border with France at Canfranc station, where he learns of the role the old terminus played in the evacuation of Jews during the Second World War

programme.title: American History's Biggest Fibs with Lucy Worsley
programme.desc: Lucy Worsley debunks the myths behind the American Civil War, revealing how its history has been retold over the years to promote freedom and equality but also hatred and division. She reveals how Abraham Lincoln's personal views, and the behaviour of his troops towards African Americans, were not as noble as they appeared. She also learns how history was rewritten in a bid to downplay the evils of slavery, and how a 1905 blockbuster film about the Civil War relaunched the Ku Klux Klan with terrifying results

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Sara Pascoe plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo, warming up the audience and then introducing routines by Larry Dean and Gary Delaney

programme.title: Apocalypse Now: The Final Cut (1979)
programme.desc: An army captain is sent on a top-secret mission into Cambodia, where his goal is to find and assassinate a renegade colonel who has gone insane and is worshipped by his followers as a god. However, as his patrol boat journeys further upriver, the conflict and disarray surrounding him becomes increasingly disturbing and nightmarish. Extended version of Francis Ford Coppola's acclaimed Vietnam War drama - re-edited in 2019 by the director himself to mark the film's 40th anniversary. Starring Martin Sheen, Marlon Brando and Robert Duvall

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day two in Sheffield, where Kyren Wilson v Anthony Hamilton and Mark Williams v Alan McManus were scheduled to reach a conclusion

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: A look back at the first-round matches on the second day of the tournament at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the likes of John Higgins and Mark Williams were in action

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Edible Garden
programme.desc: Alys Fowler demonstrates how to grow herbs and flowers and use them as ingredients in a selection of dishes, including herb omelettes, bread with rosemary and salads. She also reveals how they are important to attract wildlife to gardens, something essential to the health and productivity of any allotment

programme.title: The A to Z of TV Cooking
programme.desc: John Torode's alphabetical journey through the BBC's food archives continues as he reaches the letter U. He highlights Heston Blumenthal's recipe for sail porridge and Nigel Slater's method for cooking a black pudding sausage roll

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Anita Rani visit Chichester Harbour, the only Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty in the UK that is managed by a harbour authority, discovering how the needs of a very busy harbour are balanced with those of nature and wildlife. While Matt is out on the water doing the daily rounds with the harbour master, Anita finds out about projects helping to protect local populations of oysters, terns and seals. Plus, Adam Henson's Highland bull Archie might not have long left the farm, but his legacy lives on

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don refreshes a container of overgrown herbs, plants alpines in a pot and revisits the shrub and perennial cuttings he took a few weeks ago. Arit Anderson travels to Kent to meet a designer who is placing sustainability and the environment at the heart of the gardens she designs. Plus, a garden in Dorset that has been laid out specifically with scent in mind and an archive report by Frances Tophill who last year visited an allotment on the Isle of Man to meet a grower who uses plants to make cosmetics and there's a second chance to meet an enthusiast on the Isle of Wight who has filled his garden with daylilies

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter reveals the container potatoes she planted in the first episode, George Anderson harvests his cabbage and blackcurrants and Chris Beardshaw checks on the progress of his box blight battle and explains the art of cloud pruning

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Day three gets under way at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, where the first-round matches continue, featuring Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and Yan Bingtao v Elliott Slessor

programme.title: The Crucible: 40 Golden Snooker Years
programme.desc: Steve Davis goes back to the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield to celebrate 40 years of the world snooker championship, talking to some of the players forever ingrained in the sport's history. The venue has become the home of snooker since the championships moved there in 1977, helping to create memorable moments and scenes of drama that live on to this day. Featuring interviews with former champions Stephen Hendry and Dennis Taylor, six-time runner-up and fans' favourite Jimmy White, and celebrity snooker addicts Richard Osman and Stephen Fry

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the first-round matches, as Ronnie O'Sullivan faces Thepchaiya Un-Nooh, and John Higgins takes on Matthew Stevens. O'Sullivan and Higgins have nine world titles between them and Higgins has reached the final here for the last three years, only to end as runner-up on each occasion, while the Rocket is aiming to make a much better fist of his challenge this time, having endured a dismal run of results in the championship in recent years

programme.title: Greece with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Part two of two. Simon travels from the Peloponnese peninsula into the mountainous north of the country. He meets local people including a group of rebel monks and conservationists caring for an injured bear, and discovers how they are coping with the political crises affecting Greece

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session in Sheffield features the concluding session of two first-round matches - Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and David Gilbert v Kurt Maflin. Robertson started last year's championship as one of the favourites following an outstanding season, but the Thunder from Down Under came a cropper against eventual runner-up John Higgins, while Gilbert achieved a career highlight by reaching the semi-final, where he also came unstuck against the Wizard of Wishaw

programme.title: The Edge (2019)
programme.desc: Documentary about England's Test cricket team between 2009 and 2013, the first and only English side to ever reach the top of the world rankings. Featuring contributions from Andrew Strauss, Kevin Pietersen, Alastair Cook, James Anderson, Stuart Broad, Monty Panesar, Graeme Swann, Jonathan Trott, Matt Prior, Ian Bell, Paul Collingwood, Steven Finn, Tim Bresnan, David Saker and Andy Flower

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Fantasy drama series about people with extraordinary abilities, including a tormented artist (Santiago Cabrera) who paints images of the future, a high-school cheerleader (Hayden Panettiere) and a young dreamer (Milo Ventimiglia) who believes he can fly. In the first episode, an Indian professor travels to New York to solve the mystery of his father's death

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Policeman Matt Parkman discovers he is the only person at the scene of a crime who can hear the muffled cries of a girl, Claire receives unwanted attention and Peter comes close to learning the truth about his brother Nathan. Elsewhere, Hiro finds a comic book starring himself and goes in search of the author, while Niki attempts to piece together what happened during her blackout. Fantasy drama, starring Greg Grunberg, Hayden Panettiere and Ali Larter

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Highlights of the evening session on day three in Sheffield, featuring Neil Robertson v Liang Wenbo and David Gilbert v Kurt Maflin

programme.title: Snooker: World Championship Extra
programme.desc: Extended highlights of the first-round matches on day three, where former champions Neil Robertson, John Higgins and Ronnie O'Sullivan were among the players in action

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Iraq
programme.desc: A look at the impact of the insurgency from the perspectives of American soldier Lt Col Nate Sassaman and Baghdadi Alaa Adel, who was 12 when she suffered life-changing injuries. Unprepared for the hostile environment he found himself in, Sassaman was pushed to the very darkest regions of his psyche. Alaa was hit in the face by shrapnel from one of the first roadside bombs planted by insurgents and intended for American forces. Looking back at that time, both question the benefits of the war in Iraq

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Cameron is concerned that charismatic locum Drew Nicholson-Heath (guest star John Barrowman) is out to expose him, so he resorts to his old tricks of playing the hero - but he is almost caught out and gaslights a colleague into thinking she made a mistake. A desperate Sacha wants to give Essie a reason to keep on living, but when she insists on preparing for the inevitable, he does something completely out of character. Kian spends the day babysitting Emma, hoping to win back Jac's heart

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Gershwin's Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heyward's song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mike Read and Paul Gambaccini present the edition first broadcast on 28 December, featuring performances by Kim Wilde, Aswad and Band Aid II. Plus, Bros, Duran Duran, the Pet Shop Boys, Michael Jackson, Phil Collins, Kylie Minogue and many others feature in a reflective look at hits from the entire decade

programme.title: Gospel According to Mica - The Story of Gospel Music in Six Songs
programme.desc: Soul singer Mica Paris examines the origins of the gospel songs she sang as a child in Lewisham, south London. She investigates why some of her contemporaries have returned to their gospel roots and reconsiders her earlier decision to embrace secular music. Mica meets the choir at Fisk University in Tennessee, who once sang for Queen Victoria, and finds out how the slavery freedom fighters used gospel to communicate. She recalls Sam Cooke and Thomas A Dorsey, who both encountered tragedy after leaving the Church, and hears of current artists, such as Stormzy, who happily embrace both church and secular music

programme.title: The Last Pirates: Britain's Rebel DJs
programme.desc: Rodney P's career as a rapper would not have been possible without the lifeblood of pirate radio airplay in the 1980s. Here, he presents an alternative history of Britain during a time of entrepreneurialism and social upheaval, with archive and music that celebrates a very different side of Thatcher's Britain. His story details how land-based pirate radio broadcasters created a platform for black music, changing the soundtrack of Britain for ever. Featuring contributions by DJs, station owners and Department of Trade and Industry enforcers

programme.title: Classic Albums
programme.desc: The making of Back to Black, the second and final studio album by singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, which was released in October 2006 to wide critical and commercial acclaim. Featuring singles Rehab, You Know I'm No Good, Back to Black, Tears Dry on Their Own and Love Is a Losing Game, the record has sold approximately four million copies

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: A Prom from July 2015 at the Royal Albert Hall, when Radio 1 DJ Pete Tong employed the Heritage Orchestra to play a selection of Ibiza classics. Jules Buckley conducts through dance masterpieces by Fatboy Slim, Eric Prydz, Shapeshifters, Robert Miles, ATB, Moby, Frankie Knuckles, Orbital, Inner City, Daft Punk, Faithless, Stardust and others, with the help of singers Ella Eyre and John Newman

programme.title: I Can Go for That: The Smooth World of Yacht Rock
programme.desc: Katie Puckrik charts the progress of the genre through the 1980s, when it became the soundtrack to America in the Reagan era, and when artists like Toto, Hall & Oates and George Benson created a technicolour second wave of a super smooth sound. In the second of two programmes, yacht session supremos Jay Graydon and Steve Porcaro recall working with Benson. Porcaro joined other LA session players to form Toto and he and Steve Lukather reveal how they brought a little yacht magic to the biggest-selling album in history - Michael Jackson's Thriller. Other contributors include Robbie Dupree and John Oates

programme.title: The Defiant Ones
programme.desc: Jimmy Iovine talks about hitting a wall and how he considered his own career shift after a particularly difficult collaboration with U2, whose tireless drive in the studio rivaled his own. By 1989, Jimmy had parlayed his production expertise into a new career as co-founder of Interscope Records, committing the label to artists such as Trent Reznor's Nine Inch Nails, Marilyn Manson, Gwen Stefani and No Doubt. Dr Dre discusses a series of calamities he experienced including personal losses on the streets and run-ins with police authorities, as well as a bitter contract dispute and a clash over management that strained Dre's relationship with Eazy-E. He also recalls landing at a crossroads and looking to make a fresh start

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: The evening session on day two at the Crucible Theatre gets under way, featuring Mark Williams v Alan McManus. Williams was the defending champion here last year, but lost in the second round to David Gilbert, who then went on to defeat Kyren Wilson in the quarter-final, to reach the semi-finals for the first time in his career

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: A tidal wave has carried Brizan's lighthouse into the forest and an arm of the sea has split the small town in two, a spectacle Lena foresaw in her dreams. The inhabitants panic, but Faust Ketchak sees it as a punishment against the devastating behaviour of people, while Lena and Ben uncover pollution on Brizan's construction site. In French

programme.title: The Last Wave
programme.desc: The town's mayor calls a meeting to discuss plans to deal with the cloud, which might have a devastating effect on the surfers. In French

programme.title: Aretha Franklin: Respect
programme.desc: A tribute to the singer known as the Queen of Soul, featuring contributions from Tom Jones, Beverley Knight, Clarke Peters and Trevor Nelson among others. Aretha was raised in Detroit where her father preached, and grew up singing gospel. Her career ignited when she signed to Atlantic in 1967 and global hits such as I Say a Little Prayer and Respect quickly established her in the soul genre, while her delivery and regal presence made her an iconic figure in the emerging Civil Rights movement. She renewed success in the 1980s when collaborating with Luther Vandross, cameoing in The Blues Brothers and then duetting with the likes of Annie Lennox and George Michael

programme.title: Gospel According to Mica - The Story of Gospel Music in Six Songs
programme.desc: Soul singer Mica Paris examines the origins of the gospel songs she sang as a child in Lewisham, south London. She investigates why some of her contemporaries have returned to their gospel roots and reconsiders her earlier decision to embrace secular music. Mica meets the choir at Fisk University in Tennessee, who once sang for Queen Victoria, and finds out how the slavery freedom fighters used gospel to communicate. She recalls Sam Cooke and Thomas A Dorsey, who both encountered tragedy after leaving the Church, and hears of current artists, such as Stormzy, who happily embrace both church and secular music

programme.title: Mahalia Jackson in Concert
programme.desc: The influential New Orleans gospel singer performs in a London concert from 1964. Songs featured include The Whole World in His Hands, Silent Night and Didn't it Rain

programme.title: TOTP2
programme.desc: Mark Radcliffe provides football fans with a chance to revisit some of the best (and worst) songs produced to celebrate the tournament's conclusion over the years. Ditties such as Manchester United's 1996 song Move Move Move, featuring a youthful Paul Scholes, David Beckham and the Neville brothers, could feature alongside the arguably more 'professional' sounding efforts of the London Community Gospel Choir, which sang Abide With Me - the FA Cup's traditional anthem - ahead of Chelsea's clash with Everton at Wembley in 2009

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Mike Read and Paul Gambaccini present the edition first broadcast on 28 December, featuring performances by Kim Wilde, Aswad and Band Aid II. Plus, Bros, Duran Duran, the Pet Shop Boys, Michael Jackson, Phil Collins, Kylie Minogue and many others feature in a reflective look at hits from the entire decade

programme.title: Gershwin's Summertime: The Song That Conquered the World
programme.desc: How George Gershwin and DuBose Heyward's song Summertime became a standard around the world. The film tells the story of its first appearance in the 1935 opera Porgy and Bess, charts how it has been reinterpreted by artists including Billie Holiday, Janis Joplin, Mahalia Jackson, Miles Davis and Ella Fitzgerald, and explores the many meanings that have been derived from it through the decades. Narrated by Pauline Black

programme.title: BBC Proms Classics
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by cellist Sheku Kanneh-Mason to look back on a Prom that made history in 2017 when Chineke!, became the first British majority BME symphony orchestra to take the Proms stage. Including music by Dvorak, Handel and Rimsky-Korsakov, and featuring Kanneh-Mason himself, soprano Jeanie De Bique and conductor Kevin John Edusei

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie Cumming concludes his journey by travelling from Barton Swing Aqueduct to Liverpool Docks - but experiences engine problems on the way

programme.title: Alex Higgins: The People's Champion
programme.desc: James Nesbitt narrates a look back at the often controversial life of the two-time world snooker champion, who was widely regarded as one of the most naturally gifted players in the history of his sport, but whose brilliance was flawed by his demons. With contributions by members of his family, as well as fellow snooker greats Jimmy White, Ronnie O'Sullivan, Steve Davis and Dennis Taylor

programme.title: Chas and Dave: Last Orders
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into Chas Hodges and Dave Peacock's successful five decades in the music industry, which has seen them share the same bill as Led Zeppelin and make a big impact on London's cultural landscape. The film blends archive performances with more recent tour footage and features contributions by Pete Doherty, Jools Holland and Phill Jupitus. Narrated by Arthur Smith

programme.title: What We Were Watching
programme.desc: Grace Dent explores the sights, sounds and schedules of the Great British Summer, an epic journey that covers everything that informs viewers attitudes to summertime. She looks back at a range of influential television, from the travel shows of the sixties and seventies that first brought the world's finest resorts into our living rooms, to Del and Rodney Trotter fooling about abroad

programme.title: Ibiza: The Silent Movie (2019)
programme.desc: Director Julien Temple and soundtrack provider Fatboy Slim offer a musical time-travelling trip through the history of dance music's most influential, charismatic and spiritual island in the Mediterranean, taking in 3,000 years of its history. Ibiza's bohemian heart is now facing its strongest challenge yet, to continue to beat strongly in the face of the ever growing annual invasion of wealthy socialites and the gentrification of the island in the name of progress
