programme.desc: A photo of Gran Gran and JoJo's mummy when she was a little girl inspires JoJo and Gran Gran to take a walk and compare old photos of their neighbourhood to see how it has changed over time.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Simon and Evie take Waffle for a walk and bump into Evie's friend Anaya and her mum, AJ. An overenthusiastic Waffle drags Simon through the park, soaking him under a sprinkler

programme.title: Ferne and Rory's Vet Tales
programme.desc: Ferne visits some reindeer who are getting a winter check-up from the vet, while Rory sees how some prickly patients are being cared for by a wildlife sanctuary vet team

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Nigel and Baby Bear invite babies and their grown-ups to explore gloves through discovery, play, song and story

programme.title: Magic Hands
programme.desc: Ashley Kendall, Aimee Campbell-Nottage, Donna Mullings and Simon Herdman present a poetry programme using a mix of sign language, the spoken word, music and animation to bring poems to life and capture the imaginations of both deaf and hearing children

programme.title: Tinga Tinga Tales
programme.desc: An African fable explaining why mosquitos make a buzzing sound when they fly

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: When Mr Hammond's precious photo of a catfish is destroyed, Andy heads to Dragon's Breath Cave, in Africa, to track down a catfish and take a new picture. He meets a cheetah family and a leopard before taking the safari-mobile underwater to continue his quest. Will Andy succeed? And will he get back to Safari World before Mr Hammond notices?

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack buys Daisy a puppy called Little Mo. Moira says no pets are allowed in the Big Hub, so Molly and Mack try to hide Little Mo, but then she escapes

programme.title: Katie Morag
programme.desc: Part two of two. Grannie Island is unhappy when Granddad Island arrives with Granma Mainland for the wedding, but the little girl succeeds in making her enjoy the celebrations

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: Sarah decides to bake a cake to celebrate Duck's birthday

programme.title: Charlie and Lola
programme.desc: After being told she is too young to learn Spanish, Lola decides to make up her own country with its very own language

programme.title: Tee and Mo
programme.desc: Tee cannot stop playing his favourite game on Mo's tablet, so Mo tries to convince him that it's just as much fun playing outside

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Tiny and Small accidentally spill green soup all over Granny's best shawl, and when they ask Cloud and the froglets to help them clean up the mess, things only end up getting worse

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines look for the perfect place to have a picnic, and invite the Wottingers to join in the fun

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain reads My Little Star by Mark Sperring

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out on a quest to discover the meaning of art. It's a long strange trip into a world of giant inflatables, edible sculptures and flying doughnuts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian steps out of the spotlight to learn another trade, trying his hand at the distinctly glamourless profession of farming - and his introduction to the job sees him mucking out the cows

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on meat, cooking a rack of Scotch lamb with roast aubergine, with guests sheep farmer Michelle Stephen and comedian Jim Smith

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the FA Cup final between Arsenal and Chelsea at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session on the second day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Mark King and John Higgins v Matthew Stevens. Ding plays his match to a finish, while four-time champion Higgins, runner-up for the last three years and always one of the men to beat, gets his campaign under way

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Chelsea (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the showpiece match from Wembley Stadium. The Gunners recorded a 2-0 victory over holders Manchester City in the semi-finals thanks to a brace by Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, and Chelsea produced an impressive performance in a 3-1 victory over Manchester United the following day. The sides also met in the 2017 final, when Arsenal prevailed 2-1, though more recently, Chelsea won 4-1 against their London rivals in the 2019 Europa League final. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces from the world of food and drink to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are Ken Hom, Ching-He Huang, Candice Brown, Atul Kochhar, Andi Oliver, Michael O'Hare, Matt Tebbutt and Olly Smith

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: David is excited for Rosa to return home after visiting Xiomara, but he starts to worry that his strict routine may be boring for her. Jacob is concerned when Nate is brought into the ED struggling to breathe. Masters guiltily remembers that he should have picked up Nate's medication as soon as the interview was over. And when Lev's family life takes a turn for the worst, he battles temptation and wanders into the arms of a man he's been messaging. Starring Jason Durr, Uriel Emil and Charles Venn

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: Mavericks: Sport's Lost Heroes
programme.desc: A profile of former heavyweight boxer David Pearce, who enjoyed success in the ring during the 1980s, but had his biggest battle outside the ring

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the second match in a three-game ODI series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Prior to the opening contest in the series, England had won their last five one-dayers at this venue, twice defeating Pakistan and the West Indies and also recording a victory over South Africa

programme.title: This Country
programme.desc: Spoof documentary presenting a wry look at the lives of two ne'er-do-well cousins living in the Cotswolds, Written by and starring Daisy May and Charlie Cooper

programme.title: Spike Island (2012)
programme.desc: Five teenage boys from Manchester are desperate to see the Stone Roses in concert during the summer of 1990. Making it to the gig at Cheshire's Spike Island means spending three days on the road, an experience that tests their friendships and forces them to consider what their future holds. Drama, starring Elliott Tittensor and Nico Mirallegro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke on Trent are sold under the hammer

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Danny Sebastian and Nick Hall help teams look for items at the Mona Showground in Anglesey, while presenter Anita Manning finds out about Portmeirion pottery

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Tudur Owen sets out on a quest to discover the meaning of art. It's a long strange trip into a world of giant inflatables, edible sculptures and flying doughnuts, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Scrum V Classics
programme.desc: Ireland v Wales. A look back at the 2008 Six Nations match between the sides at Croke Park, as Wales aimed to clinch the Triple Crown and maintain their hopes of winning the Grand Slam

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: The Story of Wales
programme.desc: Huw Edwards explores the ancient kings who shaped Wales as a nation, and how their battles with Vikings, Saxons and Normans made the local culture flourish. He then learns how the death of Wales's last native prince Dafydd ap Gruffydd was followed by a century of plague and famine, and what Owain Glyndwr's rebellion against the English Crown meant

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Dangerous Liaisons (1988)
programme.desc: A philandering aristocrat in 18th-century France schemes with his manipulative former lover to seduce a virtuous young woman. As their conspiracy becomes ever more tangled, events take an unexpected turn. Stephen Frears' Oscar-winning drama of sexual politics, based on Pierre Choderlos de Laclos' novel, starring John Malkovich, Glenn Close, Michelle Pfeiffer, Swoosie Kurtz, Uma Thurman and Keanu Reeves

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich create a Scouts-inspired garden, complete with campfire and winding path, for Thomas and Kate in Harborne, Birmingham

programme.title: War Horse (2011)
programme.desc: A farmer's son forms a close bond with a horse he trains, but is forced to give the animal up when it is sold to the British army during the First World War. The horse passes through various owners on both sides as he travels across the battlefields of France, while the boy refuses to give up on being reunited. Steven Spielberg's drama based on Michael Morpurgo's book, starring Jeremy Irvine, Tom Hiddleston and Benedict Cumberbatch

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are at Ockham and Wisley Commons in Surrey on a mission to discover why heathland habitat like this needs to be prized and protected. Margherita goes in search of the sand lizard, one of Britain's rarest reptiles, while Matt is on the hunt for the nightjar. For Adam Henson it is a time of new beginnings as he reopens the farm park to visitors and celebrates the arrival of a litter of rare piglets, while Tom Heap investigates whether brewers and hop growers can recover from the impact of coronavirus.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from Salford's MediaCityUK, and meets Blue Peter presenters past and present to discuss the contents of the time capsules buried on the show. Among the items brought in to be valued are a drawing by LS Lowry on the back of a restaurant bill, an 18th-century puzzle jug, and a collection of Second World War badges

programme.title: A Suitable Boy
programme.desc: Lata continues to pursue a romantic relationship with Kabir Durrani, but the news reaches her mother, who takes action to keep the young lovers apart. Removed to Calcutta, Lata's spirits are low, until she attends a party hosted by the sophisticated and eccentric Chatterji family. Meanwhile, Saeeda Bai and Maan's affair continues, becoming a source of gossip that threatens them both. As Maan's father rounds on him under growing political pressure, Saeeda Bai takes decisive action of her own

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Celtic v Hamilton Academical. Steven Thompson presents action from the Scottish Premiership encounter at Celtic Park. Plus, a round-up of Saturday's goals and talking points in the top flight

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The students are called into the gymnasium for an impromptu assembly, where the Eagle announces some shocking news that hits everyone hard

programme.title: Looking for Alaska
programme.desc: The friends turn their attention to solving the mystery, and when the students return from winter break the gang decides to execute a prank to end all pranks

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Jeremy Dein QC and Sasha Wass QC re-investigate the case against farm labourer William Dumbleton, who was charged with the murder of travelling watch repairman John Edmonds in 1880. The accused man didn't have any legal representation until the commencement of his trial, which lasted just one day. Dumbleton was found guilty and sentenced to death

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell travel across the country to talk to the new owners of three auction property buys. A two-bedroom terrace in Norwich, a four-bed semi in Wolverhampton and a terrace in Ilford in east London are the latest development projects they visit

programme.title: Close Calls: On Camera
programme.desc: The freak wave that capsized the boat of two young men attempting to set a world record rowing the Atlantic. Plus, the family day out in the snow that almost ended in tragedy when a man suffered an accident while sledging

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Stephanie Connell and Philip Serrell help teams search for items at the Peterborough Festival of Antiques, while Eric Knowles views a horseshoe collection at Oakham Castle, Rutland

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: The Heights
programme.desc: Difficult decisions are faced, as Renee considers her family and Sabine is texted by her ex

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single 'impossible' answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Sonali Shah heads to rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products.Sonali Shah is in rural Somerset helping a retired teacher return to her West Country roots and find her country home with a £385,000 budget. While in the county, Sonali visits a local farm which has diversified into lavender growing. With the fields in full bloom, Sonali sees how the crop is harvested and has a chance to try her hand at making traditional lavender products

programme.title: Home Is Where the Art Is
programme.desc: Nick Knowles challenges three talented artists to create beautiful artworks for people they've never met. Here, the trio arrive in Oxfordshire, turning detective around a grade-II listed cottage. It's an emotional story as the mystery buyers are looking for a joyous, uplifting piece in light of a recent cancer diagnosis

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Antique experts Christina Trevanion and Charles Hanson are on the last leg of their coast-to-coast journey from Morecambe to Newcastle. Christina buys some quirky curios, while Charles splurges on a multitude of items including a Roman-era ring that he titles the Hanson Hoard. The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: British Academy Television Awards
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts the annual ceremony celebrating the best of both British and International talent in front and behind the camera, which was postponed from its usual slot in mid-May by the coronavirus. Nominees include Mo Gilligan, Pheobe Waller-Bridge, Guz Khan, Jodie Comer, Stephen Graham, Suranne Jones, Ncuti Gatwa and Lee Mack. In accordance with current restrictions, the ceremony will take place as a closed studio, socially distanced show, with nominees invited to accept their awards virtually

programme.title: Jack Whitehall's Sporting Nation
programme.desc: Jack continues to take a light-hearted and nostalgia-filled look back at Britain's sporting history, telling the nation's story through its most iconic moments and characters. In this edition, he irreverently examines how class is woven into the DNA of British sport and how breaking its social boundaries can lead to incredible drama. Focusing on the fierce athletic rivalry of Sebastian Coe and Steve Ovett, Fred Perry taking on the Wimbledon establishment and Andrew Flintoff destroying the Australians at the 2005 Ashes, British sport may well start divided, but has the ability to bring the nation together

programme.title: Keepin 'er Country
programme.desc: There is love in the air as thousands of young single people descend on a festival dedicated to country music and romance, with the dancefloor an ideal place to meet a prospective partner. The event also sees a wedding taking place on the main stage, as well as a proposal in the audience

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Conor and Jock steal the bikes of their dreams - which turn out to be the slowest bikes in history. Sergeant Healy takes chase and eventually corners them. The lads call on the help of Mairead, who makes her way to the warehouse while devising a plan to get the pair out of bother. Comedy, starring Chris Walley, Alex Murphy, Dominic MacHale and Hilary Rose

programme.title: Peter Kay's Car Share
programme.desc: The previous episode saw Kayleigh finally admit her true feelings for John - and then step out of his car and seemingly out of his life for good when he refused to say how he felt. Now Peter Kay and Sian Gibson bring the characters back for one last time - but will they finally get together?

programme.title: Poltergeist (1982)
programme.desc: A family experiences strange events after invisible phantoms invade its home. At first the ghosts seem harmless, simply making things move by themselves. But before long, they spirit away the five-year-old daughter to their own plane of existence, forcing her parents to call on paranormal researchers to find their missing child. Horror, with JoBeth Williams, Craig T Nelson and Heather O'Rourke

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: The Great Food Guys
programme.desc: Nick Nairn and Dougie Vipond focus on meat, cooking a rack of Scotch lamb with roast aubergine, with guests sheep farmer Michelle Stephen and comedian Jim Smith

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker presents a look ahead to the FA Cup final between Arsenal and Chelsea at Wembley Stadium

programme.title: Snooker: The World Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the second session on the second day at the Crucible Theatre in Sheffield, featuring Ding Junhui v Mark King and John Higgins v Matthew Stevens. Ding plays his match to a finish, while four-time champion Higgins, runner-up for the last three years and always one of the men to beat, gets his campaign under way

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Arsenal v Chelsea (Kick-off 5.30pm). Gary Lineker presents coverage of the showpiece match from Wembley Stadium. The Gunners recorded a 2-0 victory over holders Manchester City in the semi-finals thanks to a brace by Pierre-Emerick Aubameyang, and Chelsea produced an impressive performance in a 3-1 victory over Manchester United the following day. The sides also met in the 2017 final, when Arsenal prevailed 2-1, though more recently, Chelsea won 4-1 against their London rivals in the 2019 Europa League final. With analysis from Alan Shearer and Ian Wright

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman invite another selection of famous faces from the world of food and drink to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Those standing behind the podiums this week are Ken Hom, Ching-He Huang, Candice Brown, Atul Kochhar, Andi Oliver, Michael O'Hare, Matt Tebbutt and Olly Smith

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: David is excited for Rosa to return home after visiting Xiomara, but he starts to worry that his strict routine may be boring for her. Jacob is concerned when Nate is brought into the ED struggling to breathe. Masters guiltily remembers that he should have picked up Nate's medication as soon as the interview was over. And when Lev's family life takes a turn for the worst, he battles temptation and wanders into the arms of a man he's been messaging. Starring Jason Durr, Uriel Emil and Charles Venn

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: First episode of the comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as a doting mother-of-six who constantly interferes in her children's lives. Agnes tries to solve her son Dermot's relationship problems by giving his girlfriend advice, and Grandad ends up in hospital after Dr Flynn's attempt to read his temperature goes awry

programme.title: Match of the Day: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Action from the final at Wembley Stadium, where Chelsea and Arsenal clashed, looking to provide their respective managers with their first silverware in charge of the clubs. The Blues enjoyed the stronger league campaign, but the Gunners held their rivals to a draw when the sides last met at Stamford Bridge in January

programme.title: ODI Cricket Highlights
programme.desc: England v Ireland. Action from the second match in a three-game ODI series, which took place at the Ageas Bowl in Southampton. Prior to the opening contest in the series, England had won their last five one-dayers at this venue, twice defeating Pakistan and the West Indies and also recording a victory over South Africa

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Ellie is a fashion fanatic looking for a guy who shares her passion for clothes and shoes - but will she see past the clothes with Mr Grim, Mr Snaps or Mr Waves

programme.title: Hot Property
programme.desc: Katie likes an older man who can keep her on her toes - and magicians, motorbikes and a didgeridoo are just some of the things that she has to look forward to

programme.title: Spike Island (2012)
programme.desc: Five teenage boys from Manchester are desperate to see the Stone Roses in concert during the summer of 1990. Making it to the gig at Cheshire's Spike Island means spending three days on the road, an experience that tests their friendships and forces them to consider what their future holds. Drama, starring Elliott Tittensor and Nico Mirallegro

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Chris Mason and Nina Warhurst present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Ciara McEleney wants to move her whole family to Australia for the better weather. Husband Ruairi, however, is proving to be a cloud on her new horizons. A week in Perth sees them check out the forecast for moving Down Under on a permanent basis. Presented by Nicki Chapman

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A meadow in Woodville in Derbyshire, an old mill in Stoke in Staffordshire and a terraced house in Mountain Ash in Wales are all sold under the hammer

programme.desc: Aled Jones visits London's Olympic Stadium to explore the links between Christianity and sport, and JB Gill meets Watford goalkeeper Heurelho Gomes, who is training to be a pastor

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps an expat couple who want to return from Cyprus to buy a house in Lincolnshire, and also visits a college to find out about the next generation of farmers

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: After a career serving all over the world, army veteran Jason, his wife Esther, and their four children have finally put down roots - but the trouble is their Suffolk garden is little more than a concrete car park. The couple have a budget of £3,000 to try to create a space where they can enjoy sitting out as a family. As the military is a big part of this family's life, they want to see the resulting garden incorporate some remembrance poppies. Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich head to Stowmarket to see how they can help Jason and Esther to realise their dreams

programme.title: Tide
programme.desc: A look at how the organisers of a beach horse race on a tidal island off Ireland's west coast, a surfer training on the north coast, and the skipper of a ferry between the Scottish mainland and the Isle of Skye all have to take the tide into account in planning their lives. Plus, how light-bellied Brent geese nesting in Strangford Lough must study the waves to catch their food

What will be the effect on the performing arts and on our culture after months in which the cinemas, theatres, concert halls and galleries have been shut down - with huge uncertainty about how and when they will reopen? How will this seismic shock change our culture? Will people start to consume culture increasingly in the privacy of their own homes? First shown on the BBC News Channel on Saturday 4 July.

programme.title: Global Questions
programme.desc: Is the lockdown turning back the clock on women's lives? With women appearing to bear the brunt of the economic fallout of the pandemic, and the UN describing the worldwide increase in domestic abuse as a 'shadow pandemic' alongside Covid-19, many are asking if this is the biggest ever leap backward for women. And yet female leaders at the helm of various nations have been commended for managing the crisis better than their male counterparts - does that reflect that where women are elected as leaders there is a greater presence of women in positions of power across the board?

programme.title: Geamaichean Gorach

programme.title: A-null 's a-nall

programme.title: Coig Criomagan CBBC ALBA
programme.desc: Calum Macdonald introduces a selection of his favourite facts

programme.title: Sràid nan Sgread (Scream Street)

programme.title: Seonaidh (Shaun the Sheep)

programme.title: Bannan (The Ties That Bind)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Game show, hosted by Ben Shephard, in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dan is left hopeful, Charity and Sarah overhear something, and Amy forms a plan

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fights for his life, and Adam senses Sarah is lying about her showdown with her ex. Meanwhile, Alya implores Elaine not to leave as she is the only hope of securing Yasmeen's freedom, and Ed sits down to a virtual anniversary dinner with Aggie via video call

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: A body is discovered on the Northumberland moors, and identified as a woman who made a distressed 999 call on the night of her death - suggesting she may have been abducted by someone she knew. Vera takes on the case, and discovers the victim was a controversial figure in her home town, who had a complicated relationship with her family and a number of well-hidden secrets

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Sherlock Holmes: A Game of Shadows (2011)
programme.desc: The detective investigates a series of seemingly unconnected crimes all around the world, and becomes convinced one man is behind them all - the elusive Professor Moriarty. He sets forth on an international journey to bring the evil mastermind to justice, but finds he is dealing with a foe who is his intellectual equal. Detective thriller sequel, starring Robert Downey Jr, Jude Law, Noomi Rapace and Jared Harris

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Weasel (in an animal costume) for a groundhog being hunted, and confiscates the hunter's gun.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is at the pool, he wants to start sub-marine hairdressing! But Magoo, thinks he is fishing in a lake, and starts trying to catch Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: While helping Linda clean her attic, Magoo gets shrunken and teleported into Fizz's brain. Time for a spring clean.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo takes Fizz for a toy containing top secret information, which he steals from a secret agent, but it's actually just weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel plan to enter Fizz's new condiment in a culinary contest. Magoo mistakes them for members of his band ready for a reunion.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike rescues Fluffy from the raccoon's lair but he doesn't notice that the kitty has pocketed one the racoon's favourite toys. Fluffy and the turtles are in love with their new musical toy! The indignant raccoons turn up at the Mikkelsens looking for a way to get it back. How dare someone steal their stuff! But they didn't count on how hard Fluffy and the turtles will fight to keep their new favourite toy out of the racoon's hands.

programme.desc: Beetle Buck and stick insect Buddy are the best of friends who like to collect unusual and interesting things from the strange world in which they live.

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird

programme.title: Drop Dead Weird
programme.desc: Comedy drama following the adventures of an Australian family who move to an isolated seaside village in western Ireland, with the parents having a secret to hide

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined by singer Jack Savoretti, who chats and performs. Chefs Richard Bertinet and Nisha Katona also drop by, along with pie-maker extraordinaire Calum Franklin

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare their favourite American dishes, including two varieties of fluffy pancakes, pizza base with a stuffed crust, chocolate-chip cookie ice cream sandwiches, and beans served with loaded potato skins

programme.title: Riding a Dream
programme.desc: The remarkable story of 18-year-old British Muslim woman Khadijah Mellah, and her journey to the 2019 Magnolia Cup. From her inner city riding school to one of the most prestigious horse racing events in the world, this film follows her ups and downs as she gets ready for the race of her life

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day five of the meeting, including the 3.35 Unibet Stewards' Cup and support races at 1.50, 2.25, 3.00 and 4.10. Plus, races from Newmarket at 2.05, 2.40, 3.15 and 3.50. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Mark Johnson

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Flamingo, Leopard, Flower and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts a celebrity edition of the game show, asking contestants Jonathan Ross, Tess Daly and Linda Robson to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. After the first elimination, the two remaining players battle to reach the Super Catchphrase round, where one of them could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of the blind auditions in the junior version of the singing talent contest. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: On Her Majesty's Secret Service (1969)
programme.desc: While on leave, James Bond prevents a young woman - Teresa 'Tracy' Draco - from committing suicide. Her father is Marc-Ange Draco, the head of a powerful crime syndicate who is impressed by Bond and wants him to protect his daughter by marrying her. In exchange, he offers Bond information that will lead him to master criminal and arch-enemy Ernst Stavro Blofeld, who is intent on destroying world peace by means of a lethal virus unless he receives an international amnesty for his crimes. Spy adventure, starring George Lazenby in his only outing as 007, alongside Telly Savalas, Diana Rigg, Gabrielle Ferzetti and Bernard Lee

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Moments
programme.desc: Gabriel Clarke introduces a countdown of memorable moments from the FIFA World Cup, including a look at Brazil's 1970 team and the best and worst of Argentina star Diego Maradona. Plus, a selection of goals, saves and red cards

programme.title: Babushka
programme.desc: Game show hosted by Rylan Clark-Neal in which contestants answer questions to get the chance to open a set of giant Russian nesting dolls, with some empty and others containing prizes up to £10,000

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Dino Dana
programme.desc: Dino Dana, a spunky eight-year-old palaeontologist in training eats, sleeps and breathes dinos. Eager to attend Fossil Day at the Royal Ontario Museum, Dana asks her neighbour Mr Hendrickson to take her

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo is handing out food to the neighbourhood animals when he stumbles on a gigantic hamster that Fizz has released from some parallel dimension.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo can't find the book that Linda lent him and he's ready to turn the house upside down to find it. The problem is that he's actually in Fizz's loft.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo, thinking he's at the laundromat, wanders into a candy factory, where Fizz and Weasel are creating a bubblegum that makes Fizz-shaped bubbles.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: The Tracy brothers and their friends at International Rescue set out to foil the nefarious plots of the evil Hood. An updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, this time featuring computer-animated characters on live-action modeled sets. With the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak and Thomas Brodie-Sangster, with the original Parker - David Graham - returning to voice Lady Penelope's long-serving chauffeur

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo thinks he's on a trip to Translovenia and takes shelter in a mansion owned by a vampire. In reality, this vampire is just Weasel.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: A bear has escaped from the zoo and Fizz is hell-bent on making it his pet. Magoo mistakes the bear for his niece who's come to spend the day with him.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for his best friend Harry from school, and thinks the two of them are visiting their old school.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced into action when the raccoons steal the turtles to help Freddy hold up his newly made throne. The crazy raccoon envisions himself a king and thinks the turtles are the perfect finishing touch to his scheme. Mike sneaks into the raccoons' lair to steal back his friends. With Iris's help, he intends to teach the raccoons a lesson they won't forget.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Poor Mike is bedridden. He's been struck down by a nasty cold so Iris and the turtles are forced take care of his every need, including trying to repel the raccoons from the house. Fighting raccoons isn't their specialty and Iris and the turtles quickly end up locked in the garage. Mike is forced to intervene but he's so sick and weak that he's a pushover. How can he overcome the raccoons this time? Mike's problem could be the very solution he needs to defeat the tenacious raccoons.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A piano teacher meets his match in the most inept pupil ever to tinkle an ivory - none other than Mr Bean, who has got it into his head to complete Beethoven's unfinished symphony

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson stops in at a fringe family reunion

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Ronan Keating, Nick Knowles and Claire King join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare their favourite American dishes, including two varieties of fluffy pancakes, pizza base with a stuffed crust, chocolate-chip cookie ice cream sandwiches, and beans served with loaded potato skins

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The presenter turns her attention to child-friendly baking, making a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, and snowmen cakes

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three factories in Yorkshire. It reveals how Aunt Bessie's produces millions of Yorkshire puddings a year, how Yorkshire Tea turn out teabags by the truckload and how Birds Eye workers face a race against time to get their peas from field to freezer. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues his journey around the Wales Coast Path as he travels around the wild Llyn Peninsula. He takes the helm of a 1930s sailing ship to navigate the notorious Menai Strait. This narrow stretch of water, which separates Anglesey from the rest of north Wales, has a reputation for being difficult to navigate - but luckily Sean is joined by ex-pilot boat skipper Emrys Williams. Moving on, Sean also hits the waves with adaptive-surfer Llywelyn Williams, who lost his leg in a car accident, and meets a woman who walked the entire path with her donkey

programme.title: The Voice Kids
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the final day of the blind auditions in the junior version of the singing talent contest. The Voice UK's Will.i.am returns as one of the four coaches, with McFly's Danny Jones and fellow pop stars Pixie Lott and Paloma Faith occupying the other spinning red chairs. Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: The Masked Singer US
programme.desc: More mystery celebrities battle it out in a singing contest while dressed head to toe in elaborate costumes, while a panel of more famous faces tries to guess their identities. Nick Cannon oversees the contest, as mystery singers dressed as Flamingo, Leopard, Flower and Rottweiler perform, while judges Robin Thicke, Jenny McCarthy, Ken Jeong and Nicole Scherzinger try to guess their identity

programme.title: You've Been Framed!
programme.desc: Harry Hill narrates a comical selection of mishaps filmed by viewers. A motorbike gets stuck up a tree, a woman receives an electric shock from a fly zapper and a pensioner ends up on TV - after the TV ends up on top of them

programme.title: Coronation Street: Memorable Moments
programme.desc: Comedian Jason Manford narrates a look at the classic pairings and comedy double acts from the soap that have kept viewers entertained through the decades, including Stan and Hilda Ogden, Fred Elliott and Ashley Peacock, and Steve McDonald and Lloyd Mullaney.

programme.title: The Man with the Golden Gun (1974)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the death of a world-renowned scientist working on solar technology draws him into a battle of wits with a stealthy assassin, who dispatches his victims with a golden bullet and has the British secret agent next in his sights. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland and Maud Adams

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family faces the unwanted arrival of a domineering aunt, while Larry's girlfriend also pays a visit - giving the unwelcome relative an opportunity to voice her disapproval of the whole family. Sven makes an offer that helps Louisa cope with adversity and Margo falls in love - but Gerry is more concerned with his newly acquired owlets

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary allowing a peek at the workings the Ritz, one of the most famous luxury hotels in the world. Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.title: Gone to Pot: American Road Trip
programme.desc: Christopher Biggins and Bobby George are feeling the effects of a little over-indulgence after a few too many medicated mouthfuls at Nonna Marijuana's kitchen. John Fashanu and Linda Robson, both parents themselves, meet a mum who claims medical marijuana has transformed her daughter's life and, later, the group entertain at their Hollywood base, where they meet a chef who uses marijuana in cakes

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of day five of the meeting, including the 3.35 Unibet Stewards' Cup and support races at 1.50, 2.25, 3.00 and 4.10. Plus, races from Newmarket at 2.05, 2.40, 3.15 and 3.50. With analysis from Jason Weaver, reports by Oli Bell and Matt Chapman, and commentary from Mark Johnson

Youngsters between the ages of seven and 14 display their musical talents in a bid to win a £30,000 prize to contribute to their musical education, along with a family holiday to Universal Orlando Resort

programme.title: On Her Majesty's Secret Service (1969)
programme.desc: While on leave, James Bond prevents a young woman - Teresa 'Tracy' Draco - from committing suicide. Her father is Marc-Ange Draco, the head of a powerful crime syndicate who is impressed by Bond and wants him to protect his daughter by marrying her. In exchange, he offers Bond information that will lead him to master criminal and arch-enemy Ernst Stavro Blofeld, who is intent on destroying world peace by means of a lethal virus unless he receives an international amnesty for his crimes. Spy adventure, starring George Lazenby in his only outing as 007, alongside Telly Savalas, Diana Rigg, Gabrielle Ferzetti and Bernard Lee

programme.title: Unforgettable World Cup Moments

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Magoo mistakes Fizz for his best friend Harry from school, and thinks the two of them are visiting their old school.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Mike is forced into action when the raccoons steal the turtles to help Freddy hold up his newly made throne. The crazy raccoon envisions himself a king and thinks the turtles are the perfect finishing touch to his scheme. Mike sneaks into the raccoons' lair to steal back his friends. With Iris's help, he intends to teach the raccoons a lesson they won't forget.

programme.title: Mighty Mike
programme.desc: Poor Mike is bedridden. He's been struck down by a nasty cold so Iris and the turtles are forced take care of his every need, including trying to repel the raccoons from the house. Fighting raccoons isn't their specialty and Iris and the turtles quickly end up locked in the garage. Mike is forced to intervene but he's so sick and weak that he's a pushover. How can he overcome the raccoons this time? Mike's problem could be the very solution he needs to defeat the tenacious raccoons.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: A piano teacher meets his match in the most inept pupil ever to tinkle an ivory - none other than Mr Bean, who has got it into his head to complete Beethoven's unfinished symphony

programme.title: NinjaGo: Masters of Spinjitzu
programme.desc: Having grown soft in the wake of their victory, the ninja are ill-prepared when a dangerous Serpentine sorceress from Master Wu's past resurfaces.

programme.title: Ben 10
programme.desc: Team Tennyson stops in at a fringe family reunion

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: Ronan Keating, Nick Knowles and Claire King join Martin and Roman Kemp for the Sunday morning entertainment show, featuring music, chat and a host of fun and games

programme.title: John and Lisa's Weekend Kitchen
programme.desc: John Torode and Lisa Faulkner prepare their favourite American dishes, including two varieties of fluffy pancakes, pizza base with a stuffed crust, chocolate-chip cookie ice cream sandwiches, and beans served with loaded potato skins

programme.title: Beautiful Baking with Juliet Sear
programme.desc: The presenter turns her attention to child-friendly baking, making a festive reindeer cake, a gingerbread house cake, and snowmen cakes

programme.title: Inside Britain's Food Factories
programme.desc: The documentary goes behind the scenes of three factories in Yorkshire. It reveals how Aunt Bessie's produces millions of Yorkshire puddings a year, how Yorkshire Tea turn out teabags by the truckload and how Birds Eye workers face a race against time to get their peas from field to freezer. Narrated by Siobhan Finneran

programme.title: Wonders of the Coast Path
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher continues his journey around the Wales Coast Path as he travels around the wild Llyn Peninsula. He takes the helm of a 1930s sailing ship to navigate the notorious Menai Strait. This narrow stretch of water, which separates Anglesey from the rest of north Wales, has a reputation for being difficult to navigate - but luckily Sean is joined by ex-pilot boat skipper Emrys Williams. Moving on, Sean also hits the waves with adaptive-surfer Llywelyn Williams, who lost his leg in a car accident, and meets a woman who walked the entire path with her donkey

programme.title: The Man with the Golden Gun (1974)
programme.desc: James Bond's investigation into the death of a world-renowned scientist working on solar technology draws him into a battle of wits with a stealthy assassin, who dispatches his victims with a golden bullet and has the British secret agent next in his sights. Spy adventure, starring Roger Moore, Christopher Lee, Britt Ekland and Maud Adams

programme.title: The Durrells
programme.desc: The family faces the unwanted arrival of a domineering aunt, while Larry's girlfriend also pays a visit - giving the unwelcome relative an opportunity to voice her disapproval of the whole family. Sven makes an offer that helps Louisa cope with adversity and Margo falls in love - but Gerry is more concerned with his newly acquired owlets

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Neville Chamberlain visits Downton for dinner, and is drawn into Violet's plan to save the hospital, with distressing consequences for everyone. Andy finds an unexpected confidante while helping Mr Mason move into Yew Tree Farm, Mary is uncertain if she has met her match, Edith meets Bertie Pelham in London, and Spratt comes to Denker's aid. Mrs Hughes struggles to keep Carson happy, while Mary thinks secrets are being kept from her

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: The house is made open to the public for a day to raise money for the hospital, an act that divides opinions among the family and the servants. Tensions between Violet and Cora come to a head as a decision is made about the hospital takeover, Mr Dawes has a proposition for Molesley, and Baxter is thrown into turmoil by an unexpected letter. Carson is not happy with Thomas, but all is not as it appears, while Mary and Edith hope to overcome their past and find new happiness with the men in their lives

programme.title: Inside the Ritz Hotel
programme.desc: Documentary allowing a peek at the workings the Ritz, one of the most famous luxury hotels in the world. Executive chef John Williams prepares to launch the first-ever cookbook that reveals The Ritz's most famous recipes. Penny Lancaster pops in for cocktails and head concierge Michael De Cozar is celebrated for his 46 years of service

programme.title: Long Lost Family: Born Without Trace
programme.desc: Two years ago, the programme brought together 20 foundlings from across the UK, united in their hope of finding birth relatives. Through DNA and detective work, the show's experts have been helping them find the answers they've been searching for. In this edition, Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell catch up with three of them to find out how their lives have changed since they discovered the truth about their pasts and how their new relationships are developing

programme.desc: Alan smuggles a woman up to his room, making sure the lights are turned down low so she cannot see how shabby it is - but an unscheduled appearance by her irate father soon puts paid to his dreams of romance. Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale star

programme.desc: Alan and Philip goad Rigsby into fasting for three days - and it is not long before he is stealing Vienna's cat food to stave off the pangs of hunger. With Leonard Rossiter and Richard Beckinsale

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Robert's got a new job as a GP in Yorkshire, but how will he tell Ida that they're moving away?

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A gathering at the Devon estate of Miss Marple's old school friend Lady Tressilian leads to murder when the eccentric Mr Treves tells the story of an unconvicted child killer. Miss Marple is on hand to assist Supt Mallard of the local constabulary, but not before a second victim is claimed. Mystery, starring Geraldine McEwan, with guest appearances by Eileen Atkins, Tom Baker, Paul Nicholls, Alan Davies and Greg Wise, with a cameo by former British number-one tennis player Greg Rusedski

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: It is a tense day in the Glover household when the inquest into Dave's death begins. Chris urges Frank to prove that James is his child by getting a paternity test

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Seth forgets Betty's birthday leading him to pretend he has a surprise planned. Jan apologises for hitting Kathy. Zak tidies the Dingle household after inviting Lisa for dinner

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Emily sees Ashley leaving Ken's first thing in the morning. Deirdre discovers Alec is short staffed and offers her services. Steve tells Fiona he was wrong to go off with Vicky

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve and Vicky go before the Magistrates charged with perverting the course of justice. Ken tells Kelly she must be careful about what goes on in his house

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Steve Crane takes an urgent phone call, and is shocked to discover Peter Pyke's warehouse has gone up in smoke. It soon emerges that the owner's wife had spent time behind bars for fraud, and her husband is reluctant to answer questions - leading Merton to suspect the blaze was deliberate. Meanwhile, Vernon moves into his new mansion, but has trouble keeping up with his well-to-do neighbours. Rural drama, starring James Carlton, Duncan Bell and Geoffrey Hughes

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Tom and Sybbie return to Downton, but the peace is disrupted by an unexpected visit from an old friend. Baxter is faced with a dilemma after an encounter with Sergeant Willis, while Thomas struggles to fill Carson's shoes. Anna goes to London with Mary to deal with a crisis, while her secrets seem likely to be exposed. Daisy is not happy with Cora, and Mary has an admirer

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa's hippie mother Eleanor (Louise Jameson) arrives in the village, immediately taking over Martin's kitchen to prepare a herbal tonic, which she later gives to the baby to stop his crying. A culinary rivalry develops between Bert and Mark Bridge, the landlord of the Crab and Lobster pub, at Portwenn Fun Day, with both keen to take advantage of the event by providing food for the spectators

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: PC Penhale is shocked by the unexpected arrival of his ex-wife Maggie (Julie Graham), who appears to have forgotten that they separated years ago and wants to know why he left her. Eleanor goes on a date with local fisherman Paul Hale, an old friend she has not seen since school, while Martin and Louisa finally agree on a name for their baby - James Henry. With Martin Clunes, John Marquez and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When a 16-year-old girl is found dead in her home, Brooks and Devlin cannot be sure a crime was even committed. On further investigation, an unlikely murder weapon is uncovered and a suspect identified - a recently released serial rapist. Determined the accused is guilty, Steel pursues him with every power available - pushing the boundaries of the law to the limit. Crime drama, guest starring Kevin Doyle (Drop Dead Gorgeous), with Ben Daniels

programme.title: Scott & Bailey
programme.desc: Called to the home of an elderly couple who haven't been seen for a while, Janet discovers Eunice Bevan's severed head at the bottom of the stairs, her body at the top, and her husband Joe in bed, emaciated and barely able to speak. Meanwhile, the detective puts her house on the market, admitting her relationship with Ade is over, and three months in to Rachel's marriage to traffic cop Sean, she's starting to feel a little claustrophobic. George Costigan and Nicola Walker guest star, with Lesley Sharp and Suranne Jones

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective faces a tough time while looking into a murder at a peaceful college. After suffering the familiar pangs of unrequited love, he clashes with the unpleasant college master and then unwittingly provides his main suspects with a watertight alibi. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with Robert Stephens, Amanda Burton and Philip Middlemiss

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian sleuth is asked to vet potential suitors vying for the hand of an Australian heiress and discreetly accompanies her on a rail journey aboard the Plymouth Express, only to find himself investigating a jewel theft. Mystery, with David Suchet and Julian Wadham

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: A blizzard forces the sleuthing spinster to spend a night in isolated Sittaford House, situated on a Devon moor. Without explanation, the building's owner, up-and-coming politician Clive Trevelyan, decides to spend the evening in a local hotel - only for a Ouija board to predict his murder. Mystery drama with an all-star cast, including Geraldine McEwan, Robert Hardy, Matthew Kelly, Patricia Hodge and Mel Smith

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Yasmeen wakes up after a heart attack, and Brian is annoyed that Bernie found a watch in the woods and sold it to Sarah. Meanwhile, Abi tells Ed that Aggie can use Kevin's car until hers is fixed as the neighbourhood is grateful for putting herself on the front line at the hospital

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ed is gutted that Aggie has to isolate at a hotel on their 30th wedding anniversary, and Sarah demands to know if Gary killed Rick, before he is hit by a car. Elaine calls Alya and confirms that she's prepared to give evidence against Geoff

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Gary fights for his life, and Adam senses Sarah is lying about her showdown with her ex. Meanwhile, Alya implores Elaine not to leave as she is the only hope of securing Yasmeen's freedom, and Ed sits down to a virtual anniversary dinner with Aggie via video call

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A Belgian film star arrives in London and summons her friend Poirot to unravel the mysterious threats she has been receiving, which demand the return of a priceless diamond, the Western Star. Rosalind Bennett guest stars

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Geraldine McEwan makes her last appearance as the spinster sleuth before handing over the knitting needles to Julia McKenzie. Following the death of old acquaintance Mr Rafiel, Miss Marple receives instructions drawn up prior to his demise asking her to embark on a coach tour to investigate an unspecified possible crime. It soon transpires that many of her companions are also travelling at his invitation and they are all in some way connected to a girl who went missing from a nun's sanctuary during World War Two, supposedly to be with her German lover, Rafiel's son Michael. Murder mystery, with a star-studded supporting cast including Richard E Grant, Johnny Briggs, Amanda Burton, Anne Reid, Ronni Ancona, George Cole, Will Mellor and Laura Michelle Kelly

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: First-ever episode of the popular crime drama, starring John Thaw as author Colin Dexter's Oxford detective who treats his cases as intellectual challenges. In this mystery, Morse is introduced to trusty Sergeant Lewis (Kevin Whately) and takes it upon himself to investigate the apparent suicide of a lady friend. Co-starring Gemma Jones, Patrick Troughton, James Grout and Peter Woodthorpe

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Feature-length drama. While holidaying at the plush Bertram's Hotel in London, Miss Marple is presented with a fresh challenge to her deductive powers when the reading of a will leads to a double murder. Geraldine McEwan stars as the elderly spinster with a nose for a mystery, with guest appearances by Peter Davison, Mica Paris and Francesca Annis

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor is found dead on Halloween with a stake through her heart and Hobson reveals she shared a house with the victim in her university days. A medium visits Lewis claiming to have foreseen the tragedy, and ghostly messages spelled out in fridge magnet letters start appearing in a house occupied by four students. As the detective struggles with the possibility his friend is somehow implicated in the crime, a tale of jealousy and revenge begins to unfold. Rupert Graves (Sherlock) and Niamh Cusack (Heartbeat) guest star

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Art comes to the Midsomer village of Angel's Rise with the opening of a new sculpture park, but when its launch is marred by murder, DCI Barnaby and DS Nelson are called to investigate. However, they must deal with an apparently unexplainable broken locket found near the scene of the crime, a host of suspects, and a complex web of deception. As the crime solving duo inspect further, they find they have to get creative to solve a case where art imitates death and everything has a deeper meaning

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer Parva hosts the revival of a traditional pagan festival, but tragedy strikes when curate Alex Dakin is burned alive inside a straw effigy of a woman. The body count rises as an apparent victim of spontaneous combustion, a budding photographer and a newly arrived schoolteacher are the next to be roasted alive. Keith Barron guest stars

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin are called to investigate when a man is attacked and his kidney is surgically removed. The focus quickly turns to finding the intended recipient of the organ, and the team is horrified to discover that it has been trafficked into the NHS and given to wealthy patient Joanna Woodleigh. When the case is handed over to the CPS, a conflict of interests becomes apparent as Steel and Phillips go up against George Castle in court. Denis Lawson guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin investigate the death of middle-class teenager Debbie Powell. They discover the victim had been living a secret life as a drugs mule, and was being led astray by local dealer Gerry Craig. The case has an emotional impact on Castle, who demands Steel and Phillips do everything in their power to bring the person responsible to justice. Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Bill Paterson star

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks agrees to help old friend and partner Jimmy Valentine with an undercover drug sting, but when the bust goes wrong and a dealer ends up dead, suspicion falls on the officers. As Devlin pushes for the truth, the spotlight turns on Brooks' dark past, and a 10-year-old case involving no witnesses and a large quantity of missing heroin. Guest starring Hustle's Robert Glenister, former Rising Damp star Don Warrington and Anna Chancellor (Four Weddings and a Funeral), alongside Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Freema Agyeman

programme.title: Barging Round Britain with John Sergeant
programme.desc: The broadcaster takes in the delights of the Lancaster Canal, setting out from Tewitfield. As he meanders down the waterway, John cannot resist visiting Carnforth railway station, where he re-enacts his favourite scene from the classic film Brief Encounter, some of which was filmed there. After Carnforth, the canal crosses the River Lune on its way to Lancaster, where the veteran journalist marvels at the engineering of the Lune Aqueduct. Later, he heads south to Garstang, famous for its cheeses, and finally on towards Preston

programme.desc: Text-based information service
